actual_coverage.reasoning: Both focus on multi-year durability and Perfecta growth drivers despite near-term headwinds; asks to articulate the levers underpinning the trajectory versus macro noise.
Predicted asks about Q2 yield bridge and cadence; actual asks for confidence specifically in Q4 yield acceleration to hit FY midpoint. Related cadence theme but different timing/trigger.

Predicted seeks regional forward booked LF and remaining inventory (Med/WC Mexico) to gauge reliance on close-in; actual asks remaining to book in Med, magnitude of recent impact and embedded assumptions.
Both probe booking curve/trajectory after disruption and split of close-in versus forward, including where improvement shows up (APD vs load factor).
Direct match on Perfect Day Mexico ramp cadence and structural yield uplift in Western Caribbean; Galveston angle is incremental but same answer set.
Predicted covers PD Mexico capex/timing placement; actual is a binary construction-resumption status check. Same project area but different ask.
Predicted decomposes FY net yield with Med moderation; actual asks if Europe yield shortfall should fully revert in 2027. Overlapping region but different horizon/ask.
Directly requests FY net yield decomposition by ticket/onboard, new hardware/private destination contribution, and regional (incl. Europe) assumptions.

Predicted focuses on structural vs timing NCC ex-fuel; actual asks about itinerary changes due to higher fuel and any initiative deferrals. Related cost theme but a different trigger and decision set.
Predicted asks about changing hedging posture in high fuel environment; actual asks that plus NCC ex-fuel normalization threshold (partially covered).
Both probe impact of loyalty/card and higher pre-cruise booking penetration on APD/net yield and onboard monetization behavior.
No predicted questions address North American airfare inflation and related consumer behavior.

missed_actual_themes: Specific Q4 yield confidence and phasing needed to reach FY midpoint
Status update on Perfect Day Mexico construction pause/resumption
Whether 2027 fully recovers the 2026 Europe yield shortfall
Itinerary changes or initiative deferrals in response to higher fuel prices
Impact of higher North American airfares on booking behavior
overpredicted_themes: Capital allocation cadence (buybacks vs deleveraging/dividends) and triggers
Bond issuance details, tranche terms and pro-forma leverage/interest
Icon-class marginal economics, ROIC and financing mix
TUI Cruises JV sensitivity, headwind sizing and normalization path
Royal Beach Club Cozumel and CocoCay per-guest economics specifics
Detailed Q2 NCC ex-fuel bps/dry-dock cadences
Perfecta/post-Perfecta framework and long-term EPS/CapEx envelopes beyond what was asked
summary: Coverage was solid on core themes: FY net yield decomposition, Med booking status, Perfect Day Mexico ramp, hedging strategy, and loyalty/digital monetization (7/12 covered; avg best score 2.5). Precision suffered due to many predictions on capital allocation, bond terms, Icon economics, and JV topics that did not surface in the actual Q&A (useful 41/109; precision ~0.38). Biggest misses were Q4 yield phasing confidence, PD Mexico construction status, 2027 Europe yield rebound, itinerary changes due to fuel, and the impact of higher airfares.
